questions.question: What is the difference between a discrete random variable and a continuous random variable?
options: A) A discrete random variable can take on any value, while a continuous random variable can only take on specific values.
B) A discrete random variable can only take on specific values, while a continuous random variable can take on any value.
C) Both types of random variables are the same.
D) Continuous random variables can only take on integer values.
questions.explanation: A discrete random variable can only take on specific, distinct values, whereas a continuous random variable can take on any value within a given range.

questions.question: What does a random variable represent in probability theory?
options: A) A probability distribution
B) A probability measure
C) A statistical sample
D) A population parameter
questions.explanation: A random variable represents a variable whose possible values are defined by chance events, and takes on different values according to the outcome of those events.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a discrete random variable?
options: A) The number of hours worked by an employee
B) The height of a randomly selected person
C) The number of heads obtained when flipping a fair coin 10 times
D) The area of a randomly selected region on a map
questions.explanation: A discrete random variable is one whose possible values are distinct and countable, such as the number of heads obtained when flipping a fair coin.

questions.question: What is the expected value of a continuous random variable?
options: A) The probability of the variable taking on a specific value
B) The mean value of the variable
C) The standard deviation of the variable
D) The variance of the variable
questions.explanation: The expected value of a continuous random variable is the mean value of the variable, which represents the long-run average value the variable is expected to take on.

questions.question: What type of random variable can take on a value of infinity?
options: A) Discrete random variable
B) Continuous random variable
C) Both discrete and continuous random variables
D) Neither discrete nor continuous random variables
questions.explanation: A continuous random variable can take on any value within a given range, including infinity, whereas a discrete random variable can only take on specific, distinct values.
